examples: The scarp has slopes with grades greater than 60 degrees.
Old scarp — Waste recovered from products used by consumers.
New scarp — waste composed of the residual materials generated during production.
At dusk that second day I followed a hollowed path that wound up the wooded scarp slope of the downs east of Storrington.
In 1760, a young aristocrat from the scarp-foot village of Wiston named Charles Goring decided to add his own layer of history to the Chanctonbury earthworks.
